novelty: measured-Tc-zero (group-17 lightest halide); §9.15 FAIL below predicted 50-100K (-38%)
purpose: Group-17 lightest-X (F) analog of H3Cl. Tests electronegativity-extreme limit — F is most electronegative; expected to over-localize bonding electrons and damage Fermi-surface DOS for superconductivity.
system: H3F cubic Im-3m BCC primitive (1 F + 3 H = 4 atoms), celldm=5.12696 bohr (a≈2.713 Å), PBE PAW

method.scf: ecutwfc=60 Ry, ecutrho=600 Ry, 24x24x24 k-grid, MP smearing degauss=0.02 Ry
method.phonon: ph.x DFPT ldisp 6x6x6 q (16 irreducible, weights [4,12,12,12,12,12,8,8,12,12,12,8,12,8,12,4]=160), electron_phonon='simple', el_ph_sigma 0.005-0.050 Ry (10 broadenings)
method.moments: BZ-weighted lambda + lambda-weighted log-average omega_log; Allen-Dynes Tc with mu*=0.10 and 0.13

ALIGNN_per_cand.pathology_note: ALIGNN AD-from-a2F Tc=1.2K vs DFT 31-33K — 26× underprediction. ω_log_ML=80K vs DFT 660K — 8× off. Halide out-of-distribution.

section_9_15_verdict.broadening_monotonicity: λ_BZ nearly flat (0.807 → 0.822 across 0.015→0.030) — WELL CONVERGED. Result is robust.
section_9_15_verdict.hypothesis_electronegativity_damage: F's extreme electronegativity (3.98 Pauling) over-localizes H-F bonding charge → reduces N(E_F) Fermi-surface DOS → lambda collapse (0.8 vs Cl's 1.1-1.4). Predicted by §9.15 hypothesis.

honest_observations: Lowest λ_BZ (~0.81) among 5 candidates — confirms electronegativity-damage hypothesis (F most-electronegative anion). Well-converged: Tc 31-33K robust.
ω_log 652-670K is intermediate — stronger F-H bond softens H-vibration mode (counterintuitive vs Cl's 1250K).
Anti-monotone halide series: lighter F is WORSE than heavier Cl (105-134K) — distinct from group-16 monotone λ↑/ω_log↓ trend.
citations: RTSC.md §9.15 (H3X group sweep — F FAIL below band, electronegativity-damage hypothesis)
